items.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder and largest individual shareholder of Fermi America, Inc., has proposed a town hall webinar for June 30th where he, Governor Rick Perry, and other parties will answer questions from analysts and journalists about the company's past, present, and future. Neugebauer is seeking to give shareholders a say in the company's direction, particularly regarding Project Matador, a 7,500-acre private power campus in Texas, and whether to pursue a strategic dual-track process to maximize shareholder value. The proposed webinar comes after Governor Perry and the board gave themselves a 70% supermajority vote threshold, making it difficult for shareholders to change course.

Neugebauer, along with other participants, has issued a press release reaffirming his support for John Sellers of Double Eagle as Chairman and CEO of Fermi, citing the need for enhanced accountability and credibility to prevent value destruction by the current Board and management team. Neugebauer claims that the current Board has failed to replace nine key senior executives who departed Fermi after his termination and has ignored proposals from large companies that could offer greater value to shareholders. The release also highlights Neugebauer's intent to continue his campaign to seat a new, strong, independent Board and restore Fermi's position as a pro-shareholder REIT.

items.summary: Fermi Inc is mailing a Consent Revocation Statement to shareholders in response to a consent solicitation by former CEO Toby Neugebauer, who is seeking to call a Special Meeting of Shareholders. The company's Board of Directors urges shareholders to sign and return the WHITE Consent Revocation Card to protect their investment and support the company's continued execution of its Fermi 2.0 strategy. Neugebauer, who was terminated for cause, currently does not have the required support to call a Special Meeting, and the company emphasizes that it is making progress on its projects, including Project Matador, and is focused on maximizing long-term shareholder value.

items.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder of Fermi Inc., is soliciting shareholder support to call a special meeting to vote on expanding the board of directors and electing seven new candidates, including himself, and removing three current directors. The proposed changes aim to install a new board majority that would consider a potential sale or strategic partnership of the company, which Neugebauer believes could maximize value for shareholders. Neugebauer's effort is a second attempt to provide shareholders with a vote on the company's future after his initial attempt was obstructed by the company.

items.excerpt: SAN FRANCISCO , Jan. 21, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- National shareholder rights firm Hagens Berman is notifying investors in Fermi Inc. (NASDAQ: FRMI ) of the upcoming March 6, 2026 , lead plaintiff deadline in a pending securities class action. Hagens Berman is investigating the alleged claims that Fermi IPO materials and subsequent disclosures misled investors regarding tenant demand and funding for its flagship "Project Matador" AI campus. Investors who purchased Fermi (FRMI) securities pursuant and/or traceable to the company's October 2025 IPO, or between Oct. 1, 2025, and Dec. 11, 2025 , and suffered significant losses are encouraged to contact the firm.

items.summary: /PRNewswire/ -- Fermi America, a pioneer in gigawatt‑scale private grid infrastructure for hyperscale AI, and Westinghouse Electric Company are collaborating...
items.excerpt: AMARILLO, Texas , Aug. 21, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Fermi America, a pioneer in gigawatt‑scale private grid infrastructure for hyperscale AI, and Westinghouse Electric Company are collaborating to finalize the Combined Operating License Application (COLA) to deploy four advanced modular AP1000 ® reactors at Fermi America's Amarillo hyperscale campus. Under the agreement, the companies will partner to complete the COLA documentation that was submitted by Fermi America to the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) on June 17, 2025 , and support the review process for approval. In addition, the companies will work together to develop a long-term deployment strategy for the site.
